---
title: "Parse Alignment"
---

### Overview

This pipeline performs combinatorial indexing processing using the Parse Biosciences split-pipe (v1.3.1) software. It orchestrates the transition from raw FASTQ multiplexed libraries to a Digital Gene Expression (DGE) matrix.

***

### Workflow Logic

The pipeline follows a tiered execution strategy to manage heavy I/O and storage constraints.

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    A[get_refs] --> B[make_ref]
    C[cat_fqs] --> D[run_parse]
    B --> D
    D --> E[run_parse_combine]

    style D fill:#f96,stroke:#333
    style E fill:#f96,stroke:#333
```

1. `get_refs`: Download the hg38 reference files (Ensembl Release 113).
2. `make_ref`: Prepares genome indices for split-pipe.
3. `cat_fqs`: Combine fastq files per sublibrary (across lanes), per read (R1, R2).
4. `run_parse`: Core alignment and barcode demultiplexing.
5. `run_parse_combine`: Aggregates sublibraries into a unified plate-level DGE.

***

::: {.callout-warning}

Environment Clash Workaround

There was a conflict between the Snakemake YAML parser and the spipe environment.
Fix: The spipe-1.3.1 environment is manually activated within the shell block of the rule rather than via the Snakemake conda: directive.
:::

### Requirements


+ `Software`: [Parse](https://www.parsebiosciences.com) `split-pipe` software we used v1.3.1
+ `Conda environment`: Parse `split-pipe` software and virtual environment: I used v1.3.1
+ `Input files`: demultiplexed fastq files 
+ `Genome references`: We used [ENSEMBL release 113](https://www.ensembl.info/2024/10/18/ensembl-113-has-been-released/) 
+ A `sample list` (for each plate)
+ A `json file` listing where lane- and sublibrary-specific fastqs are located. Use `scripts/create_parse_json.py` to generate this. 

***

### Library Composition & Throughput

We processed 150 samples across three sequencing plates:

+ Sublibraries: 43 total (Plate 1: 15, Plate 2: 16, Plate 3: 12).
+ FASTQ Depth: Each sublibrary consists of 24 files (4 lanes × 3 runs × 2 reads).
+ Data Volume: ~3.0 TB of final raw output (~1 TB per plate).

***

### Resource Management Strategy

To navigate a 5TB storage quota and heavy memory requirements, the following engineering strategies were implemented:

+ Concurrency Control: Restricted to 3 parallel jobs to prevent storage overflow.
+ I/O Optimization: Combined FASTQ transfer and concatenation into a single step to minimize scratch disk residency.
+ Cleanup Logic: Intermediate merged FASTQs were deleted immediately upon successful alignment.
+ HPC Allocation: Executed on DPMCN nodes (c_compute_neuro1) to avoid prohibitive queue times on Hawk.

***

### Compute profile

+ Threads: 32
+ Memory: 360 GB
+ Walltime: 10 Days

*** 

### Known Issue

There wass a clash between the snakemake environment and the spipe environment.
Workaround: We manually activate `spipe-1.3.1` within the shell block rather than using the `conda` directive.
